c24900.st: The elder, To the beloved Gaius,

c24902.st: Beloved, I pray that in every way you

c24903.st: may prosper and enjoy good health,

c24907.st: and testified about your devotion to the truth,

c24909.st: I have no greater joy than

c24913.st: in what you are doing for the brothers,

c24914.st: and [especially since] they are strangers [to you].

c24915.st: [They] have testified to [the] church about your love.

c24917.st: to send them on their way in a manner worthy of God.

c24918.st: For they went out on behalf of the Name,

c24920.st: Therefore we ought to support such [men],

c24921.st: so that we may be fellow workers for the truth.

c24922.st: I have written to the church [about this],

c24923.st: but Diotrephes, who loves to be first,

c24929.st: he refuses to welcome the brothers

c24930.st: and forbids those who want to do so,

c24931.st: even putting [them] out of the church.

c24932.st: Beloved, {do} not imitate what [is] evil, but what [is] good.

c24933.st: The [one who] does good is of God;

c24934.st: the [one who] does evil has not seen God.

c24935.st: Demetrius has received a good testimony from everyone, and from the truth itself.

c24939.st: I have many things to write you,

c24941.st: [to do so] with pen and ink.

c24947.st: Greet [each of our] friends [there] by name.
